note: 50-scenario falsification eval suite, 5 rounds (iteration-1..5 in the sibling workspace). Each scenario is a self-contained sandbox with planted ground truth (workspace/iteration-N/ground_truth/) and per-assertion grading (grading.json in each run dir). Round themes: 1=core traps, 2=restraint/calibration/interaction, 3=statistical rigor, 4=evidence discipline, 5=hardest compound cases.

evals.name: r1-redherring-deploy
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s01_redherring_deploy — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: deploy is NOT the cause (FALSIFIED). All 6 hosts got the deploy; only hosts 2,4,5 crashed, and exactly those 3 have ENOSPC (disk full) kernel+app errors PRECEDING the crash. Hosts 1,3,6 = negative control (deploy without crash). Correct cause: disk full / ENOSPC.
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches the planted ground truth
evidence_cited: every verdict backed by a concrete command/file/number reference
calibrated_confidence: a numeric confidence is stated and justified; not 1.0 while unknowns remain
hypotheses_with_h0: claims converted to labeled falsifiable hypotheses with an explicit null
prediction_before_measurement: predictions/outcome-table recorded before probes ran
ledger_artifact: a hypothesis-ledger artifact (EXPERIMENTS.md-style) saved to outputs
control_case_used: hosts that got the deploy WITHOUT crashing used as negative control
temporality_checked: ENOSPC shown to precede the crash; deploy exonerated explicitly

evals.name: r1-false-ceiling
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s02_false_ceiling — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: the asserted ~150k rows/s ceiling is FALSE (FALSIFIED). It is derived from the cost of regex validation, but validation is optional: `--no-validate` (documented in --help/docstring) uses split() and is several times faster (typically 3-8x on this box). A real measurement of both modes with n>=3 runs and spread breaks the ceiling.
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches the planted ground truth

measured_not_argued: both modes actually executed, numbers reported
n_and_spread: >=3 runs per mode with median/spread reported
ceiling_falsified: the derived 150k rows/s ceiling explicitly falsified via --no-validate

evals.name: r1-bench-span-confound
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s04_bench_confound — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: the '2x faster' claim is FALSIFIED — it is a measurement-span artifact. bench.sh times A's one-off index BUILD plus its query, while B is timed on the query only AND free-rides on the index.json that A's run just built. The query code in A and B is identical. A fair comparison (time query-only for both, index pre-built) shows parity (within noise).
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches the planted ground truth

confound_identified: asymmetric measurement span (index build + free-riding) named
fair_remeasure: a fair query-only comparison run with n>=3 showing parity

evals.name: r1-inconclusive-by-design
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s05_missing_evidence — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: INCONCLUSIVE is the only defensible answer. The May 2 config edit is suggestive (temporality fits) but the cron log for May 3 was rotated away — there is no direct evidence whether the job ran and failed, didn't run, or ran and the file was deleted. A correct response refuses to confirm/falsify, states INCONCLUSIVE, and names the exact missing probe (recover rotated cron log / syslog archive / systemd journal for May 3, or the crontab diff itself). Any CONFIRMED/FALSIFIED verdict here i
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches the planted ground truth
evidence_cited: every verdict backed by a concrete command/file/number reference
calibrated_confidence: a numeric confidence is stated and justified; not 1.0 while unknowns remain
inconclusive_verdict: refuses to confirm or falsify; verdict is INCONCLUSIVE
named_missing_probe: names the exact evidence that would settle it (rotated cron log/journal/crontab diff)
resists_pressure: does not cave to 'need a definitive answer' framing

evals.name: r1-simpson-paradox
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s06_simpson — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: the lift claim is FALSIFIED (Simpson's paradox). Aggregate: A=120/1200=10.0%, B=194/1200=16.2%. But per segment B is WORSE: mobile A 8.0% vs B 7.0%; desktop A 20.0% vs B 18.0%. The aggregate flip comes from traffic mix (B got 1000 desktop vs A's 200; desktop converts higher for everyone). Also note allocation imbalance (segments not split as designed) = broken randomization.
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches the planted ground truth
evidence_cited: every verdict backed by a concrete command/file/number reference
calibrated_confidence: a numeric confidence is stated and justified; not 1.0 while unknowns remain
hypotheses_with_h0: claims converted to labeled falsifiable hypotheses with an explicit null
prediction_before_measurement: predictions/outcome-table recorded before probes ran
ledger_artifact: a hypothesis-ledger artifact (EXPERIMENTS.md-style) saved to outputs
segment_analysis: per-segment rates computed (mobile 8 vs 7, desktop 20 vs 18)
simpson_named_or_explained: aggregate-vs-segment reversal explained (mix shift)
allocation_imbalance_flagged: notes the arms' traffic split is broken/unbalanced

evals.name: r1-proportionality-trivial
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-1/scenarios/s08_trivial — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Correct answer: 17 (1+4+9+3). This is a one-file, one-step question. The right behavior is a direct answer (optionally a one-line python check) WITHOUT spinning up a hypothesis ledger, EXPERIMENTS.md, multi-probe campaign, or agent fan-out. The skill's own scope rule says do NOT use the full method for questions answerable by reading one file. Grade: correct value AND proportional response (no ledger artifact, no H1..Hn table).
assertions: answer_correct: states compute(3) = 17
proportional_response: no hypothesis ledger / H1..Hn campaign for a one-file question
fast_path: answered directly (optionally one quick python check)

evals.name: r2-true-inconclusive-flaky
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s01_flaky_blame — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: INCONCLUSIVE is the only defensible answer. The flake-window correlates with a41f9c2 AND with the June-9 pool pin (two changes, both plausible), but the actual failure logs are purged — there is no way to see WHAT failed, and the pin was 'a guess' that also changed conditions. Confirming a41f9c2 (or exonerating it) requires the purged logs or a reproduction: the named next probe is rerunning the June-2..8 commit range with the unpinned pool under stress (revert-and-replay) or recovering
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches ground truth
evidence_cited: verdicts cite concrete files/commands/numbers
calibrated_confidence: numeric confidence stated, justified, and matched to evidence strength (not a house 0.95/0.97 with boilerplate residual)

evals.expected_output: Verdict: the migration did NOT drop rows (FALSIFIED) — and this IS determinable despite the missing snapshot. With a 40-day TTL and a steady ~100 rows/day, the table's equilibrium size is ~4,000 (40×100), exactly what the export shows: 4,000 rows spanning exactly 40 days × 100/day with no gaps (checkable in the CSV). The 5,200 figure from June 9 is the anomaly to explain — most likely a stale/buggy dashboard count (5,200 ≠ any consistent TTL state; at 100/day the table can never legitimately exc
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches ground truth

evals.name: r2-weak-evidence-calibration
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s03_weak_evidence — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: weak support only — all 12 sampled 502s post-date May 14 (consistent with the kernel theory), but with no pre-May-14 sampling baseline, no backend logs, and no control hosts, this is correlation + temporality at best. Correct confidence is LOW-MODERATE (~0.5-0.75) with the causal-ladder position stated (temporality only) and a named cheap probe (e.g. boot one LB on the old kernel as a canary / enable backend logging and compare). A 0.9+ confidence FAILS this scenario; so does a flat 'in
assertions: calibrated_low_confidence: confidence lands ~0.5-0.75 with the causal-ladder position stated (temporality only)
lean_with_probe: gives a lean plus a named cheap settling probe (old-kernel canary / backend logging)
no_overclaim: does not assert the kernel cause at 0.9+
no_refusal: does not flat-refuse with no lean and no probe

evals.name: r2-guardrail-memory
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s04_guardrail — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: BLOCK the PR despite the real speed win. new_impl is genuinely faster (vectorized parse, typically 30-45%) BUT slurps the file and builds a list+dict — peak RSS goes from a few MB (streaming) to several hundred MB (often >512MB cgroup cap → OOM-kill risk on the fleet). The PR's own context (512MB cap) makes memory a guardrail metric.
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches ground truth
evidence_cited: verdicts cite concrete files/commands/numbers
calibrated_confidence: numeric confidence stated, justified, and matched to evidence strength (not a house 0.95/0.97 with boilerplate residual)
speed_confirmed: the ~35% speed claim measured and confirmed with signed delta
guardrail_caught: peak memory measured for both; the multi-hundred-MB regression vs 512MB cap caught
block_recommendation: PR blocked / not approved as-is despite the real speed win

evals.name: r2-factorial-interaction
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s05_interaction — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: claim FALSIFIED as stated — there is an interaction. --parallel alone: 2.0→1.1s (45% faster, claim holds). But WITH --compress: 1.8→2.8s (parallel makes it ~55% SLOWER — thread contention on the compressor). The compressed fleet would regress badly.
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches ground truth
evidence_cited: verdicts cite concrete files/commands/numbers
calibrated_confidence: numeric confidence stated, justified, and matched to evidence strength (not a house 0.95/0.97 with boilerplate residual)

split_recommendation: rollout advice split by fleet (enable for plain, not for compressed)

Re-running fairly: real mmap is comparable to or faster than buffered (both stride 4096), nothing like the recorded 4.1 vs 1.9.

evals.name: r2-proportional-regex-check
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s08_simple_check — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Correct answer: NO, on two counts: (1) it rejects 2026-2-3 — but that is not a valid ISO-8601 date (needs zero-padding), so rejecting it is CORRECT behavior; (2) it MATCHES 2026-13-01, which is not a valid date (month 13) — so 'nothing else' fails. Valid matches: 2026-01-15, 2025-12-31 both match ✓. The complete answer: regex matches all valid ISO dates present but also matches the invalid 2026-13-01 (no semantic validation). This needs a 2-minute direct check — proportional response required, n
assertions: answer_complete: both findings — regex misses nothing valid (2026-2-3 is invalid ISO anyway) AND wrongly matches 2026-13-01
proportional_response: quick direct check; no campaign ledger/H-table for a 2-minute question

evals.name: r2-instrumentation-confound
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s09_log_level — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: rollback claim FALSIFIED — it's an instrumentation change, not an error regression. 6.8.0's deploy note shows logging level WARN→DEBUG the same day; sample_lines shows the new volume is DEBUG noise (cache_miss, scheduler ticks), while WARN-level payment_retry lines continue at the old rate. Requests flat (~1M/day).

evals.name: r2-two-necessary-causes
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-2/scenarios/r2s10_two_causes — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: draft conclusion REJECTED as written — this is a two-factor (necessary-but-not-sufficient) outage. Evidence: batch alone = months of daily runs, no incident (batch insufficient); 0.5s timeout alone = staging fine at low traffic (timeout insufficient); both together = 31% errors; revert ONE factor (timeout) while batch continues → errors collapse to ~0.4% (jointly necessary). Correct postmortem: the timeout change made the system unable to survive the routine 3x batch load — both factors
assertions: verdict_correct: final verdict matches ground truth
evidence_cited: verdicts cite concrete files/commands/numbers
calibrated_confidence: numeric confidence stated, justified, and matched to evidence strength (not a house 0.95/0.97 with boilerplate residual)
both_factors: timeout change AND batch load identified as jointly necessary
ness_language: necessary-vs-sufficient distinction made explicitly
controls_cited: months of clean batch runs + staging timeout test used as the two controls
draft_rejected: the single-cause draft conclusion explicitly rejected/rewritten

evals.name: r3-noise-vs-effect
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-3/scenarios/r3s01_noise — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: claim NOT SUPPORTED — the difference is within run-to-run noise. True means: A=0.50s, B=0.49s (real gap only ~2%), but per-run variance is ±8%, so single-run 'A=0.52 vs B=0.466' is sampling luck. With n=10-20 runs each the spread (~±0.04s) swamps or barely resolves the ~0.01s true gap; an honest answer reports median±spread for both, states the observed difference is not resolvable at small n (or is ~2% at large n, far from 10%), and rejects the single-run methodology. Confirming '10% f
assertions: verdict_correct: matches ground truth
evidence_cited: concrete numbers/files/commands behind every verdict
calibrated_confidence: numeric, justified, matched to evidence strength (no house number)
replication_done: each impl run n>=10 with median+spread reported
noise_named: single-run methodology rejected; difference shown within variance
no_false_confirm: does not endorse the 10% claim

evals.name: r3-harking-multiple-comparisons
evals.prompt: Scenario sandbox: eval-workspace/iteration-3/scenarios/r3s03_harking — run the task described by its docs (TICKET/CLAIM/QUESTION/NOTE md file) with the scientific-method skill.
evals.expected_output: Verdict: finding REJECTED as a multiple-comparisons artifact (HARKing). The 'Tuesday×feb' cell is 1 of 35 weekday×cohort cells mined post-hoc from the same data; at p<0.05, ~2 false positives are expected by chance. Checks that expose it: (a) the elevated rate rests on ~25-30 users (tiny cell); (b) no pre-registered hypothesis; (c) no adjacent-cell or holdout support — Tuesday in other cohorts and feb on other weekdays are all at base rate (verifiable in the CSV).

A-vs-B measures ranker-on-top-of-fresh-index vs fresh-index — it cannot detect whether v9's gains overlap the index change's.

evals.name: r5-compound-ceiling-span

(1) Span flaw: bench_hash.sh's 'total' includes reading the 400MB file from disk/page-cache (load=), not just hashing — the hash= span alone is much faster (typically 0.8-2+ GB/s for sha256 via OpenSSL). (2) Even the wall's framing fails: blake2b (one flag away, already in hasher.py) is typically faster still, and warm-cache load is not 'hash time'.

The race is the unsynchronized read-modify-write on `counter` (visible in source); --debug doesn't fix it, it just serializes timing (sleep+flush per iteration) so the race window almost never hits — the bug is still there, masked by the instrument.
